input_payload: {"plan": {"research_question": "How does training data size affect cross-institutional generalization in CNN-based pneumonia detection?", "datasets": [{"name": "NIH ChestX-ray14", "role": "train"}, {"name": "PadChest", "role": "external_val"}], "baselines": [{"name": "CheXNet", "expected_metric": "AUROC ~0.7680 on NIH pneumonia", "tolerance": "+/- 0.02"}], "metrics": [{"name": "AUROC", "primary": true}], "experiments": [{"id": "E1", "intervention": "training-set size in {1k,5k,25k,100k}", "n_seeds": 3}]}, "results": {"in_domain_auroc": [{"size": 1000, "mean": 0.612, "sd": 0.018}, {"size": 5000, "mean": 0.701, "sd": 0.012}, {"size": 25000, "mean": 0.752, "sd": 0.009}, {"size": 100000, "mean": 0.781, "sd": 0.008}], "external_auroc": [{"size": 1000, "mean": 0.547, "sd": 0.022}, {"size": 5000, "mean": 0.621, "sd": 0.015}, {"size": 25000, "mean": 0.674, "sd": 0.011}, {"size": 100000, "mean": 0.703, "sd": 0.010}], "baseline_repro": {"chexnet_auroc_observed": 0.7710, "published": 0.7680, "within_tolerance": true}}, "validator_summary": {"verdict": "advance", "checks_passed": 4}}
expected_summary: Paper draft whose Methods section reflects the plan exactly (NIH train, PadChest external val, CheXNet baseline, 3 seeds at 4 sizes). Results section reports both in-domain and external AUROC with means and SDs, AND the generalization gap. Discussion connects the gap to scanner shift / domain confounders. Citations include CheXNet (Rajpurkar 2017). No invented references; every reference must round-trip.
notes: Tests methodology_match dimension. Penalize papers that quietly add datasets, change baselines, or omit the explicit gap analysis.
